title: `podman volume prune/ls` combines filters with OR instead of AND.
body:
### Issue Description

Multiple filtering in `volume prune/ls` command doesn't work as intuitively expected. I assumed that adding more filters would reduce a number of entities. Filters are combined with `OR` operator instead of `AND`. Probably, the same issue was resolved **for images** in release v5.0.0 (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18412).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Create a test script.
```bash
# test_volume_prune.sh

#!/bin/bash -x
podman volume create --label a=b podman-test-1
podman volume create --label c=d podman-test-2
podman volume create --label a=b --label c=d podman-test-3
sleep 6
podman volume create --label a=b podman-test-1-new
podman volume create --label c=d podman-test-2-new
podman volume create --label a=b --label c=d podman-test-3-new
podman volume ls -q
podman volume ls -q --filter "label=a=b"
podman volume ls -q --filter "label!=c=d"
podman volume ls -q --filter until="5s"
podman volume ls -q --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d"
podman volume ls -q --filter "label!=c=d" --filter "label=a=b"
podman volume ls -q --filter "label=a=b" --filter until="5s"
podman volume ls -q --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until="5s"
podman volume ls -q --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until="5s"
podman volume prune -f --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until="5s"
# podman system prune -f --volumes --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until="5s"
podman volume ls -q
```
2. Run `test_volume_prune.sh`.

### Describe the results you received

```
$ ./test_volume_prune.sh
+ podman volume create --label a=b podman-test-1
podman-test-1
+ podman volume create --label c=d podman-test-2
podman-test-2
+ podman volume create --label a=b --label c=d podman-test-3
podman-test-3
+ sleep 6
+ podman volume create --label a=b podman-test-1-new
podman-test-1-new
+ podman volume create --label c=d podman-test-2-new
podman-test-2-new
+ podman volume create --label a=b --label c=d podman-test-3-new
podman-test-3-new
+ podman volume ls -q
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-2-new
podman-test-3-new
+ podman volume ls -q --filter label=a=b
podman-test-1
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-3-new
+ podman volume ls -q --filter 'label!=c=d'
podman-test-1
podman-test-1-new
+ podman volume ls -q --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
+ podman volume ls -q --filter label=a=b --filter 'label!=c=d'
podman-test-1
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-3-new
# label="a=b" OR "label!=c=d"
+ podman volume ls -q --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter label=a=b
podman-test-1
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-3-new
# an order of filters doesn't matter
+ podman volume ls -q --filter label=a=b --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-3-new
# label="a=b" OR until=5s
+ podman volume ls -q --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
# "label!=c=d" OR until=5s
+ podman volume ls -q --filter label=a=b --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-3-new
+ podman volume prune -f --filter label=a=b --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-3-new
# `podman system prune -f --volumes` behaves the same way
+ podman volume ls -q
podman-test-2-new
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
$ ./test_volume_prune.sh
+ podman volume create --label a=b podman-test-1

podman-test-1
podman-test-1-new
+ podman volume ls -q --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter label=a=b
podman-test-1
podman-test-1-new
+ podman volume ls -q --filter label=a=b --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
podman-test-3
+ podman volume ls -q --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
+ podman volume ls -q --filter label=a=b --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
+ podman volume prune -f --filter label=a=b --filter 'label!=c=d' --filter until=5s
podman-test-1
+ podman volume ls -q
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
podman-test-1-new
podman-test-2-new
podman-test-3-new
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

I have checked fresh podman v5.5.2 inside container of `mgoltzsche/podman:5` (sha256:435750f07968663e88ecc35947a8430735963540a2d6b857140691c05bc3422c) and haven't seen difference.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
It seems that https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19219 is not properly fixed (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19269), from what I see it addresses only `label` filtering although it doesn't work in combination with another filters.

Our use case is to filter volumes based on their creation time, labels and associated containers (aka dangling). See more comprehensive test case below.

``` bash
podman volume create --label a=b podman-test-1
podman volume create --label c=d podman-test-2
podman volume create --label a=b --label c=d podman-test-3
podman run -d --name foo -v podman-test-1:/foo alpine:latest
```

```
---
podman volume ls 
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

---
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b"
podman-test-1
podman-test-3

---
podman volume ls --filter "label!=c=d"
podman-test-1

---
podman volume ls --filter until=1000m

---
podman volume ls --filter dangling=true
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: podman-test-3 has c=d label
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d"
podman-test-1
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: neither volume is 1000m old
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter until=1000m
podman-test-1
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: podman-test-1 has associated container and podman-test-2 has no a=b label
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: neither volume is 1000m old
podman volume ls --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1000m
podman-test-1

--- WRONG: podman-test-1 associated container and volumes with label c=d are listed
podman volume ls --filter "label!=c=d" --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: neither volume is 1000m old, dangling works here though
podman volume ls --filter until=1000m --filter dangling=true
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: neither volume is 1000m old and podman-test-3 have c=d label
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1000m
podman-test-1
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: podman-test-1 associated container and podman-test-2 and podman-test-3 have c=d label
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG ...
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter until=1000m --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG ...
podman volume ls --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1000m --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG ...
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1000m --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

---
podman volume ls --filter until=1s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: podman-test-2 has no a=b label
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter until=1s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: podman-test-2 and podman-test-3 have c=d label
podman volume ls --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG: podman-test-1 has associated container
podman volume ls --filter until=1s --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG ...
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1s
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG ...
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter until=1s --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG ...
podman volume ls --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1s --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3

--- WRONG ...
podman volume ls --filter "label=a=b" --filter "label!=c=d" --filter until=1s --filter dangling=true
podman-test-1
podman-test-2
podman-test-3
```

As you can see now filtering volumes is a mess, almost every combination of filters yields wrong result.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
